message:
Your dog's nose is basically a superpower. 🐶👃

Dogs carry up to 300 million scent receptors — we humans get by with about 6 million. That's why a pup can smell a single treat at the bottom of your bag from across the room (and know exactly when you're hiding one).

Even cooler: dogs can keep smelling while they breathe out, so the stream of scent never stops. A quick sniff of a lamppost is your dog scrolling through the entire neighborhood's news feed.

So next time your dog freezes to sniff something on a walk, give them a minute — they're not being slow, they're catching up on everything that happened all day. 💛
